holdings.companyName: Aditya Birla Sun Life Gold ETF

ltcg_boundary_30d.title: STCG → LTCG conversion coming
ltcg_boundary_30d.body: 6 holdings will convert from STCG to LTCG within 30 days. This can significantly reduce your tax.

ltcg_boundary_30d.title: STCG vs LTCG in mutual funds
ltcg_boundary_30d.body: Equity funds held >12 months qualify for LTCG tax at 12.5% (above ₹1.25L exemption). STCG is taxed at 20%. Waiting a few weeks could save you meaningful tax.

ltcg_boundary_30d.title: Holdings approaching LTCG
ltcg_boundary_30d.body: These holdings are days away from the LTCG threshold. Consider waiting before redeeming.

ltcg_boundary_30d.title: Tax savings potential
ltcg_boundary_30d.body: The difference between STCG (20%) and LTCG (12.5% with ₹1.25L exemption) can save you thousands — or lakhs on larger holdings.

ltcg_boundary_30d.title: Wait before redeeming
ltcg_boundary_30d.body: If you were planning to redeem these funds, waiting a few more days could qualify them for the lower LTCG rate.

regular_plan_cost.title: You're paying hidden commissions
regular_plan_cost.body: 3 of your funds are Regular plans. Distributors earn a commission from your returns every year.

regular_plan_cost.title: Regular vs Direct plans
regular_plan_cost.body: Mutual funds come in two versions: Regular (with distributor commission baked into the TER) and Direct (lower TER, you keep the difference). Over 10+ years, the difference compounds significantly.

regular_plan_cost.title: Your commission leak
regular_plan_cost.body: Your Regular plan funds have a combined value of 6.61L. Switching to Direct could save you ~0 per year in fees.

regular_plan_cost.title: 10-year compounding effect
regular_plan_cost.body: That annual savings, reinvested over 10 years at your portfolio's XIRR, would grow to a significant amount. The longer you wait, the more you lose to compounding drag.

regular_plan_cost.title: Switch to Direct plans
regular_plan_cost.body: You can switch each fund from Regular to Direct through your AMC's website or apps like Kuvera, Groww, or Coin. The switch is a redemption + re-purchase — check for exit load and tax impact first.

tax_harvest_seasonal.title: Tax harvesting season is here
tax_harvest_seasonal.body: It's Jan-Mar — the perfect window to book LTCG up to your remaining ₹67,789 exemption and reinvest immediately.

tax_harvest_seasonal.title: What is tax harvesting?
tax_harvest_seasonal.body: Tax harvesting means booking gains within your LTCG exemption limit and reinvesting immediately. You "reset" your purchase price higher while paying zero tax.

tax_harvest_seasonal.title: Your harvesting opportunity
tax_harvest_seasonal.body: You have ₹67,789 of unused LTCG exemption this FY. Book gains up to this amount tax-free before March 31.

tax_harvest_seasonal.title: Why this matters
tax_harvest_seasonal.body: Tax harvesting every year reduces your future tax liability. Over 10 years, annual harvesting can save you lakhs in taxes.

tax_harvest_seasonal.title: Harvest before March 31
tax_harvest_seasonal.body: Redeem units with LTCG up to your remaining exemption, then reinvest the proceeds. This resets your cost basis without any tax impact.

no_nominees.title: Some folios have no nominee
no_nominees.body: 5 of your folios don't have a nominee registered. This can cause major issues for your family during emergencies.

no_nominees.title: Why nominees matter
no_nominees.body: Without a nominee, your family would need to go through a lengthy legal process to claim your mutual fund investments. SEBI has made nomination mandatory for new folios.

no_nominees.title: Folios without nominees
no_nominees.body: 5 folios missing nominee details. This is a critical gap in your financial planning.

no_nominees.title: What happens without a nominee
no_nominees.body: Claims without a nominee require succession certificates, legal heir certificates, or probate — a process that can take 6-12 months and involve legal costs.

no_nominees.title: Add nominees now
no_nominees.body: Log into your AMC websites or visit a CAMS/KFintech service centre. You can also update nominees through MFCentral.com. It's a one-time 5-minute process per folio.

elss_unlock_30d.title: ELSS units unlocking soon
elss_unlock_30d.body: 1 ELSS holding with oldest units completing the 3-year lock-in within 30 days. Note: each SIP installment has its own 3-year lock.

elss_unlock_30d.title: ELSS lock-in period
elss_unlock_30d.body: ELSS has a mandatory 3-year lock-in per installment. If you invest via SIP, each month's units unlock 3 years from their purchase date — not all at once.

elss_unlock_30d.title: Unlocking soon
elss_unlock_30d.body: The oldest units in your ELSS investments are about to complete their lock-in period. Newer SIP installments may still be locked.

elss_unlock_30d.title: Redeem or hold?
elss_unlock_30d.body: If the fund is a consistent performer, holding beyond lock-in is often wise. If underperforming, you can switch to a better ELSS (for next year's 80C) or a non-ELSS fund.

elss_unlock_30d.title: Review performance at unlock
elss_unlock_30d.body: Compare your ELSS fund's 3-year return against its benchmark and category average. Decide based on performance, not just because the lock-in ended.

sip_missed.title: SIP installments missed
sip_missed.body: 5 funds with missed SIP installments. Irregular SIPs reduce the benefit of rupee cost averaging.

sip_missed.title: Why SIP consistency matters
sip_missed.body: SIPs work through rupee cost averaging — buying more units when prices are low and fewer when high. Missing installments, especially during market dips, defeats this purpose.

sip_missed.title: Missed SIPs
sip_missed.body: Some of your SIP schemes have irregular patterns. This reduces the compounding benefit of disciplined investing.

sip_missed.title: Cost of missed SIPs
sip_missed.body: Missing even 2-3 SIP installments during a market dip can reduce your 10-year wealth by 5-8%. Consistency beats timing.

sip_missed.title: Ensure SIP mandates are active
sip_missed.body: Check your bank mandates for these funds. Set up auto-debit with sufficient balance. Consider a backup bank account for SIP debits.

too_many_funds.title: You own too many funds
too_many_funds.body: 19 active funds is more than you need. Research shows 5-7 well-chosen funds provide optimal diversification.

too_many_funds.title: Diminishing diversification
too_many_funds.body: Beyond 7-8 funds, additional funds rarely add diversification. Instead, they increase overlap, complexity, and tracking burden.

too_many_funds.title: Your fund count
too_many_funds.body: You have 19 active funds. Some likely overlap significantly, diluting your returns and making tracking harder.

too_many_funds.title: Portfolio simplification
too_many_funds.body: Fewer, well-chosen funds typically outperform bloated portfolios. Less overlap means lower effective expense ratios.

too_many_funds.title: Consolidate to 5-7 funds
too_many_funds.body: Identify overlapping or underperforming funds. Keep the best in each category and redirect SIPs. Use our overlap analysis to guide consolidation.

portfolio_neglect.title: Some funds haven't been reviewed in a year
portfolio_neglect.body: 4 funds with no activity for over a year. An annual review ensures your portfolio stays aligned with your goals.

portfolio_neglect.title: Annual portfolio review
portfolio_neglect.body: Even buy-and-hold portfolios need an annual check. Fund performance can deteriorate, categories can change, and your goals may evolve. A quick review prevents silent underperformance.

portfolio_neglect.title: Holdings due for review
portfolio_neglect.body: These funds have had no transactions in over a year. This doesn't mean they're bad — but they deserve a performance check.

portfolio_neglect.title: Silent underperformance
portfolio_neglect.body: Funds that underperform their benchmark for 4+ quarters rarely recover. Early detection saves your returns.

portfolio_neglect.title: Review dormant funds
portfolio_neglect.body: Check each fund's rolling 1-year return vs its benchmark. If it consistently trails, consider switching to a better alternative in the same category.

investor_profile.title: Your investor profile
investor_profile.body: Based on your transaction history, we've identified your investing personality. This helps us personalize your insights.

investor_profile.title: Investor archetypes
investor_profile.body: Every investor has patterns — SIP discipline, reaction to market drops, holding period tendencies. Understanding yours helps you make better decisions.

investor_profile.title: Your pattern
investor_profile.body: We analyzed your transaction history to identify your investing behavior. Your profile will be refined as we observe more data.

investor_profile.title: Self-awareness edge
investor_profile.body: Investors who understand their own behavioral biases tend to make 2-3% better annual returns by avoiding emotional decisions.

investor_profile.title: Lean into your strengths
investor_profile.body: Your investor type comes with natural strengths. We'll tailor future insights to reinforce your good habits and gently flag risky ones.

risk_reward_monthly.title: Your risk-reward profile this month
risk_reward_monthly.body: Sharpe ratio: 0.70. Max drawdown: -27.04%. Your portfolio's risk-adjusted returns at a glance.

risk_reward_monthly.title: Understanding Sharpe ratio
risk_reward_monthly.body: The Sharpe ratio measures return per unit of risk. Above 1.0 is good, above 2.0 is excellent. A negative Sharpe means you'd have been better off in a savings account.

risk_reward_monthly.title: What this means for you
risk_reward_monthly.body: A good Sharpe ratio means your returns are compensating you fairly for the risk taken. Low Sharpe with high volatility suggests you could get better risk-adjusted returns elsewhere.

risk_reward_monthly.title: Optimize risk-reward
risk_reward_monthly.body: If your Sharpe ratio is below 1, consider whether lower-volatility alternatives (like index funds or balanced funds) could deliver similar returns with less risk.

benchmark_weekly.title: Your weekly benchmark check
benchmark_weekly.body: Your portfolio XIRR is 9.64%. Here's how you compare to major indices this week.

benchmark_weekly.title: XIRR vs CAGR
benchmark_weekly.body: Your portfolio XIRR accounts for the timing and size of each investment. CAGR is a simpler measure for lump sum returns. XIRR is more accurate for SIP investors.

benchmark_weekly.title: Portfolio vs benchmarks
benchmark_weekly.body: Your portfolio is delivering 9.64% XIRR. Compare this against the benchmark returns to gauge your performance.

benchmark_weekly.title: What this means
benchmark_weekly.body: Beating the benchmark consistently suggests your fund selection is adding value. Trailing it may mean index funds could do better for less cost.

benchmark_weekly.title: Review underperformers
benchmark_weekly.body: If your portfolio trails the benchmark, identify which funds are dragging performance. Consider switching persistent underperformers.

best_worst_fund_weekly.title: Your best & worst this week
best_worst_fund_weekly.body: Best: Aditya Birla Sun Life Gold Fund at 71.76%. Worst:  at -3.19%.

best_worst_fund_weekly.title: Short-term vs long-term
best_worst_fund_weekly.body: Weekly performance is noise. Don't make buy/sell decisions based on one week. Look at rolling 1-year and 3-year returns for meaningful signals.

best_worst_fund_weekly.title: Performance spread
best_worst_fund_weekly.body: The gap between your best and worst fund is 75.0 percentage points. A wide spread isn't necessarily bad — it can mean genuine diversification.

best_worst_fund_weekly.title: Why both matter
best_worst_fund_weekly.body: Your best fund drives returns, your worst teaches you about risk tolerance. Both are important for portfolio design.

best_worst_fund_weekly.title: Check trailing 1Y return
best_worst_fund_weekly.body: If a fund has been your worst performer for 4+ consecutive quarters, it might be worth reviewing its fundamentals.

actionables.title: Harvest LTCG before March 31
actionables.description: You have ₹67,789 of unused LTCG exemption. Book gains tax-free before the financial year ends.

actionables.title: Switch Regular plans to Direct
actionables.description: 3 funds worth ₹6,60,885 in Regular plans. Switching to Direct could save ~₹0/year in commissions.

actionables.description: 1 ELSS holding will unlock within 90 days. Review performance and decide whether to hold, switch, or redeem.

actionables.title: Review dormant holdings

actionables.title: Step up your SIPs
actionables.description: 4 SIPs running at the same amount for 12+ months. Consider a 10% annual step-up to keep pace with inflation.

whatIfScenarios.name: Worst Fund → Best Fund
whatIfScenarios.description: What if you had invested in SBI Gold Fund instead of ?

dataPointsForNarrative.framing: Your worst fund ( at -27.4% XIRR) cost you ₹2,53,237. If that money had been in SBI Gold Fund (149.8% XIRR), you'd have ₹3,59,727 instead of ₹1,06,490.

whatIfScenarios.name: FD vs Mutual Funds
whatIfScenarios.description: What if you had put all your money in a bank FD at 7% instead?

dataPointsForNarrative.framing: Your mutual funds earned ₹94,460 more than an FD would have
At your current pace (₹1,34,995/mo SIP + 9.6% returns), you'll hit ₹50 Lakh by Mar 2027 — 1 years away

whatIfScenarios.name: Coffee Money SIP
whatIfScenarios.description: What if your daily ₹200 coffee money was a SIP instead?

dataPointsForNarrative.framing: Your daily ₹200 coffee over 3 years could have grown to ₹2,55,718 as a SIP

whatIfScenarios.name: Inflation Reality Check
whatIfScenarios.description: Your ₹1,61,575 gain is really only ₹-3,46,842 after inflation

dataPointsForNarrative.framing: Inflation ate ₹5,08,417 of your gains — your real return is 3.4% vs nominal 9.6%

whatIfScenarios.name: Top Fund in Category
whatIfScenarios.description: What if each fund had been the top performer in its category?

dataPointsForNarrative.framing: If you had picked category toppers, your portfolio could be ₹6,89,353 more. The biggest gap was 28.4 percentage points (SBI PSU (G))
